mnemonics.title: Exception Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: Think of 'TEF' - Throwable is the Empire, Error and Exception are its children. Exception has Runtime (Runners) and other regular soldiers (checked). Remember: T → E (Error) & E (Exception)

mnemonics.title: Try-Catch-Finally Flow
mnemonics.content: T-C-F: Try = Attempt action, Catch = Handle problem, Finally = Always do cleanup. Like: Try to drive, Catch if you crash, Finally clean your car.

mnemonics.title: Throw vs Throws
mnemonics.content: Throw = Action (throw a ball), Throws = Declaration (raises hand to signal). Throw inside method, Throws in method signature.

mnemonics.title: Checked vs Unchecked
mnemonics.content: Checked = Compiler CHECKS (forced to handle), Unchecked = Not checked by compiler. Remember: IOException, SQLException = Checked (Checked = Checked by compiler).

acronyms.expansion: Test Resource Yourself - Put code that might fail in try block
acronyms.usage: Used to enclose code that might throw exception

acronyms.expansion: Code to Analyze Trouble Cause Here - Handles specific exceptions
acronyms.usage: Used to catch and handle exceptions thrown in try block

acronyms.expansion: Finally Important Cleanup Logic - Always executes
acronyms.usage: Used for cleanup code that must execute regardless of exception

acronyms.expansion: Java Virtual Machine - Handles uncaught exceptions
acronyms.usage: When exception is not caught, JVM terminates and prints stack trace

visualTips.visualization: Draw an inverted triangle: Top = Throwable, below it two branches (Error left, Exception right), Exception splits into RuntimeException (left) and other Exceptions (right)
Think of a funnel: Try at top (wide), catches as narrow tubes for specific exceptions, finally at bottom as always-open drain
Stack of plates: Exception starts at bottom, if not caught, moves up to plate above (caller method), continues until caught or falls off top (JVM)
Series of filters: First filter catches specific exceptions, general catch is final filter that catches everything else

analogies.analogy: Like a safety net in circus: performer (code) tries act, if falls (exception), safety net (catch) catches them, paramedics (finally) always check on performer after.
Checked exceptions are like required insurance - you must show it before driving (compile time). Unchecked are like speeding - only caught if happens (runtime).
Passing a hot potato: If you don't catch (handle) the exception in your method, you pass it to your parent (caller). It keeps passing until someone catches it or it burns the whole family (JVM).
